themes.heading: Muut turvallisuustason tiedot
themes.text: Noudata erityistä varovaisuutta: Abuja ja Lagos Vältä kaikkea matkustamista: Borno, Yobe, Adamawa, Gombe, Katsina, Zamfara, Niger, Kaduna, Imo sekä Niger-joen suistoalue; Deltan, Bayelsan, Riversin, Akwa Ibomin ja Cross River -osavaltioden joki- ja suoalueille, joihin pääsee veneellä, mutta ei maanteitse.

themes.heading: Ajankohtaista
themes.text: Kehotamme maassa olevia pysymään valppaina ja noudattamaan erityistä varovaisuutta. Yleinen turvallisuustilanne koko maassa jatkuu epävakaana. Yhteiskunnallisen liikehdinnän ml. protestien riski on kohonnut ja jatkunee vuoden 2027 parlamentti- ja presidentinvaaleihin saakka. Maan koillis-, luoteis- ja keskiosissa turvallisuustilanne on erityisen heikko, johtuen valtion turvallisuusjoukkojen ja erinäisten terroristi- ja rikollisjoukkojen väkivaltaisista yhteenotoista. Suureen osaan maata on voimassa matkustusrajoituksia johtuen väkivaltaisen rikollisuuden, terrorismin, aseellisten hyökkäysten, kidnappausten ja yhteiskunnallisten levottomuuksien vaarasta. Lähi-idän tilanne vaikuttaa myös välillisesti Nigerian turvallisuustilanteeseen. Terrori-iskujen uhka on kohonnut. Suosittelemme välttämään väkijoukkoja, seuraamaan paikallisviranomaisten tiedotteita, sekä laatimaan henkilökohtainen turvallisuussuunnitelma hätätilanteiden varalle. Kansainvälisten pankki- ja luottokorttien käyttöä ei suositella kopiointiriskin vuoksi, eikä kansainvälisen luottokortin käyttö monesti ole edes teknisesti mahdollista. Nigerian naira on suljettu valuutta, eli valuuttaa ei voi vaihtaa ennen maahan saapumista. UM:n matkustustiedotteet ovat suosituksia Vastaat matkapäätöksestäsi aina itse. Paikallisilla viranomaisilla on ensisijainen vastuu kaikkien maan alueella oleskelevien ihmisten turvallisuudesta ja hätätilanteissa etsintä- ja pelastustoimista. Kriisitilanteissa noudata paikallisten viranomaisten ohjeita. Seuraa uutisointia tiedotusvälineissä sekä tiedotusta edustuston verkkosivuilla ja sosiaalisen median kanavissa. Ulkoministeriö ja Suomen ulkomaanedustustot avustavat suomalaisia konsulipalvelulain mukaisesti. Mikäli Euroopan unionin ulkopuolisessa kohdemaassa ei ole Suomen edustustoa, voit kääntyä muiden EU-maiden edustustojen puoleen. Matkatoimiston järjestämällä matkalla olevaa avustaa ensisijaisesti matkanjärjestäjä. Konsulipalvelulaki Konsulisuojeludirektiivi Laki matkapalveluyhdistelmistä Suosittelemme aina matkavakuutuksen ottamista. Tarkista ennen matkaasi matkavakuutuksesi kattavuus ja voimassaolo erityisesti jos sinulla on jokin sairaus, olet raskaana, matkasi suuntautuu vaarallisille alueille tai harrastat riskialttiita lajeja, Sairastuneen tai vainajan kotiuttaminen on kallista ja vaikea järjestää ilman kattavaa matkavakuutusta, eikä hoitoa aina saa ulkomailla ilman maksusitoumusta tai ennakkomaksua. Tee matkustusilmoitus osoitteessa https://matkustusilmoitus.fi/

themes.heading: Yleinen turvallisuustilanne
themes.text: Korruptio, köyhyys, heikot instituutiot ja vähäinen luottamus yhteiskunnan toimivuuteen, maan huono taloustilanne (pikään jatkunut korkea inflaatio) ja laajalle levinnyt epävarmuus sekä näköalattomuus ovat uhka yhteiskunnalliselle vakaudelle. Nigerian yleinen turvallisuustilanne on heikko. Vaikka pääkaupunki Abujaa ja liike-elämän keskusta Lagosia pidetään yleisesti muuta maata vakaampina ja turvallisempina, tulee niissäkin noudattaa erityistä varovaisuutta. Koko maassa terrori-iskun mahdollisuutta ei voida sulkea pois. Nigerian yleinen turvallisuustilanne on heikko. Rikollisuus ja kidnappaukset ovat mahdollisia koko maassa. Viranomaisten toimintakykyyn ei voi kaikissa tilanteissa luottaa. Rikollisuus on kriittisellä tasolla kaikkialla Nigeriassa. Yleisimmin raportoituja rikoksia ovat ryöstöt, aseelliset ryöstöt, pahoinpitelyt, petokset, kiristykset, kidnappaukset, raiskaukset ja asuntomurrot. Lunnaita vaativia kidnappauksia tapahtuu usein ja ne kohdistuvat myös varakkaiksi koettuihin länsimaalaisiin. Yli 47 % kansasta elää köyhyydessä ja yheiskunnallinen tilanne on jännittynyt. Protestit ja mielenosoitukset johtuen mm. polttoaine-, ruoka ja valuuttapulasta ovat verrattain yleisiä, myös Abujassa. Yleisesti julkiset kokoontumiset ovat pysyneet rauhallisina ja aiheuttaneet ainoastaan vakavia liikennehäiriöitä ja viivästyksiä. Ajoittaiset Islamistisen liikkeen (IMN) järjestämät mielenosoitukset Abujassa ovat olleet väkivaltaisia; ajoneuvoja on poltettu ja mielenosoittajat varautuneet teräasein. Nigeriassa toimii useita väkivaltaisia ääri- ja rikollisliikkeitä. Terrori-iskujen uhka on korkea etenkin maan pohjois-, koillis- ja luoteisosissa, mutta iskut ovat mahdollisia koko maassa. Terroristien kohteena on pääsääntöisesti Nigerian liittohallitus tai osavaltiohallitus, joten riski iskuille on korkein julkisissa tiloissa, kuten julkishallinnon rakennuksissa ja kouluissa sekä massatapahtumissa. Terroristit voivat kuitenkin hyökätä varoittamatta myös ostoskeskuksiin, toreille, hotelleihin, uskonnollisiin rakennuksiin, lentoasemille ja muihin paikkoihin, joihin väkijoukot kokoontuvat. Maanviljelijöiden ja paimentolaisten kilpailu elintilasta on johtanut väkivaltaisuuksiin maan keskiosassa. Etelä- ja Kaakkois-Nigeriassa epävakautta aiheuttavat kapinaliikkeet ja separatistinen liikehdintä, mutta myös rikollisjengit. Aseistetut jengit ovat kohdistaneet väkivaltaisuuksia myös maan turvallisuuspalveluihin. Suurin osa kansainvälisestä matkustuksesta suuntautuu pääkaupunki Abujaan ja liike-elämän suurimpaan keskukseen Lagosiin, joissa liikkuminen päiväsaikaan on suhteellisen turvallista ja yleisimmin käytettyjen korkeatasoisten hotellien sekä julkisten rakennusten turvatoimet ovat yleensä tiukat. Paloturvallisuus on heikko kaikissa kiinteistöissä ja tulipalot muodostavatkin merkittävän uhan liikenteen lisäksi. Kiinteistöihin asennettuja palovaroittimia ei välttämättä ole huollettu asianmukaisesti ja hätäpoistumistiet voivat olla esteellisiä. Sähkönjakelu on epävarmaa vuoden ympäri. Sähkökatkoja esiintyy päivittäin ja jännite on usein epätasainen. Huonokuntoiset sähköjärjestelmät aiheuttavat myös merkittävän tulipaloriskin. Nigeriassa on myös jatkuva pula polttoaineista, joten varsinkin kriisitilanteissa sähkönsaanti voi loppua nopeastikin. Sähköteknikka on pääsääntöisesti huonolaatuista ja sähkövirta epätasaista, joten erilaisten vikavirtasuojien käyttöä suositellaan.

themes.heading: Rikollisuus
themes.text: Vaikka väkivaltarikosten, kuten ryöstöjen, aseellisten ryöstöjen, asuntomurtojen, kidnappausten ja raiskausten riskit ovar merkittävästi korkeammat Abujan ja Lagosin kaupunkialueiden ulkopuolella, myös turvallisempana pidetyillä alueilla on syytä valppauteen. Varsinkin pienemmät ryöstöt ja nujakat ovat yleisiä - myös kaupunkien keskusta-alueilla ja päiväsaikaan. Suurin osa väkivaltarikoksista tapahtuu kuitenkin aamuöisin, ja kaikkea liikkumista yöaikaan on vältettävä. Väkivaltaisia ryöstöjä, joissa uhria uhataan terä- tai pienaseella tai pippurisuihkeella tapahtuu yleisesti massatapahtumissa, kuten konserteissa. Kaikkia massatapahtumia tuleekin välttää edellä mainituista syistä. Lisäksi massatapahtumissa on aina riski laajemmille nujakoille ja toisaalta poliisiväkivallalle, terrori-iskuille ja hallitsemattomille tulipaloille. Erilaisten huijausyritysten mahdollisuuteen tulee suhtautua vakavasti. Huijauksia saattaa tulla esim. sähköpostin muodossa niin yrityksille kuin yksityisille henkilöille. Näihin viesteihin ei tule vastata. Korruptio on Nigeriassa yleistä. Jos passisi katoaa tai varastetaan, tee siitä ilmoitus paikalliselle poliisiasemalle. Mikäli kohdemaassa ei ole Suomen edustustoa, hae hätämatkustusasiakirjaa (ETD = Emergency Travel Document) toisen EU-maan edustustosta. Ota kopiot tärkeimmistä asiapapereista kuten passista ja lentolipuista. Säilytä ne erillään alkuperäisistä.

themes.heading: Liikenne
themes.text: Liikenne on korkea turvallisuusriski. Tieliikennesääntöjä noudatetaan vähän, jos lainkaan. Onnettomuudet ovat yleisiä. Väkivaltarikollisuuden uhka maantiekuljetusten aikana on korkea, mukaan lukien sieppaukset ja aseelliset ryöstöt. Suurin osa väkivaltarikoksista tapahtuu aamuyön tunteina, ja liikkumista yöllä tulee välttää. Poliisin/armeijan tarkastuspisteet ovat yleisiä pääteillä ilta- ja yöaikaan, ja myös niissä on tapahtunut väkivaltatilanteita ja aseellisia yhteydenottoja. Tarkastuspisteissä saatetaan kysyä rahalahjaa. Autojen ovet tulee pitää lukittuina autoa ajaessa ja kyydissä ollessa. Paikallisen kuljettajan ei voi luottaa ymmärtävän turvallisuustilannetta länsimaalaisen näkökulmasta. Ulkona liikuttaessa pitää aina olla valpas ja liikkumista yksin tuntemattomilla ja syrjäisillä alueilla on vältettävä myös päiväsaikaan. Lentokenttien turvallisuus Nigeriassa on huono ja siksi monet ulkomaiset lentoyhtiöt suorittavat oman turvatarkastuksen ennen matkustajien koneeseen nousua. Lentokoneiden huolto ja tarkastus eivät kaikilta osin täytä kansainvälisiä vaatimuksia. Maansisäisten lentojen aikataulut eivät usein pidä paikkaansa, ja lentojen peruuntuminen on yleistä. Lentäminen on siitä huolimatta usein varteenotettavin matkustustapa. Esimerkiksi pääkaupunki Abujasta ei suositella lähdettävän ulos muulla tavoin kuin lentämällä. Mikäli välttämättömistä syistä näin on tehtävä, maanteitä pitkin matkustettaessa on suositeltavaa käyttää yksityisen turvafirman palveluja.

themes.heading: Luonnonolot
themes.text: Nigeiassa on karkeasti jaoteltuna kaksi vuodenaikaa: sadekausi (touko-marraskuu) ja kuivakausi (joulu-huhtikuu). Ympäri vuoden on lämmintä. Kaikkein viileimpinä aikoina yöt voivat olla viileähköjä osissa maata. Kuivan kauden aikana Saharalta puhaltava Harmattan-tuuli tuo hiekkapölyä, heikentäen ilmanlaatua huomattavasti. Hiekkapöly saattaa rasittaa silmiä ja hengitysteitä erityisesti allergisilla ja astmaatikoilla. Hiekkapöly vaikeuttaa myös näkyvyyttä, esim. liikenteessä. Sadekauden aikana voimakkaat ukonilmat ja rankkasateet ovat tavallisia. Niiden aiheuttamat tulvat voivat katkaista tieyhteyksiä myös Abujassa ja Lagosissa.

themes.heading: Terveys
themes.text: Kansainvälisen rokotuskortin esittämiseen tulee varautua maahan saavuttaessa. Keltakuumerokotus on pakollinen. Sairaaloiden taso on heikko ja hoitoon pääsy hätätilanteessa voi olla vaikeaa. Trooppisista taudeista mm. keltakuume, lassakuume, polio, kolera, lavantauti ja malaria ovat yleisiä. Myös HIV/Aids tilanne on vaikea. Malariaa esiintyy yleisesti, joten sopivasta malarianestolääkityksestä kannattaa huolehtia ennen matkaa. Sadekautena malarianestolääkitys on erityisen tarpeellinen. Nukkumista hyttysverkoilla suojaamattomissa tiloissa kannattaa välttää, erityisesti jos ilmastointia ei ole käytettävissä. Hyttysten pistoilta kannattaa suojautua myös käyttämällä hyttyskarkotteita ja peittäviä vaatteita. Kaikki säännöllisesti käytettävät lääkkeet tulisi varata mukaan matkan ajaksi, sillä apteekeissa myytävien lääkkeiden laatuun ei voi luottaa. Puhtaan juomaveden saanti ei ole itestäänselvyys ja lisäksi hyvään käsihygieniaan on tärkeää kiinnittää huomiota. Selvitä ennakkoon matkakohteesi tartuntatautitilanne ja rokotussuositukset. Löydät ne seuraavien viranomaisten verkkosivuilta: Matkailijan terveysopas Maailman terveysjärjestö WHO Euroopan tartuntatautivirasto

themes.heading: Maan lait ja käytännöt
themes.text: Nigeriassa homoseksuaalisuus on rikos. Julkisia hellyydenosoituksia myös vastakkaiseen sukupuoleen on hyvä välttää julkisuudessa. Pukeutumiskoodi on usein verrattain konservatiivinen, erityisesti muslimialueilla. Trooppisesta ilmastosta huolimatta erityisesti uskonnollisissa ja julkishallinnon rakennuksissa sekä joissain ravintoloissa edellytetään peittävämpää, tilanteeseen sopivaa pukeutumista. Hallinnollisten rakennusten (ml. liitto-. osavaltio-, ja paikallishallinto) sotilaskohteiden, lentokenttien, siltojen ja diplomaattisten kohteiden , kuten suurlähetystöjen, valokuvaaminen on Nigeriassa lailla kielletty. Näiden paikkojen välittömässä läheisyydessä ei tule esiintyä ilman lupaa kameran tai tallennuslaitteiden kanssa. Myös droonien maahantuonti voi vaatia erillisiä lupaprosesseja. Varmista tarvittaessa tarkemmat rajoitukset etukäteen paikallisviranomaisilta. Kiellettyjä kohteita kuvaava henkilö saatetaan ottaa kiinni tai pidättää, ja kuvauslaitteet saatetaan tuhota tai takavarikoida. Nigeria tietoliikenneyhteyksissä on häiriöitä päivittäin. Suomalaiset SIM-kortit eivät toimi maassa välttämättä lainkaan. Roaming-maksut ovat poikkeuksellisen kalliita. Paikallista SIM-korttia ei saa hankittua ilman nigerialaista henkilötunnusta. Yksi vaihtoehto on ostaa matkan ajaksi eSIM kansainväliseltä palveluntarjoajalta. Laadukkaat hotellit tarjoavat asiakkailleen usein toimivan wifi-yhteyden. Heikon kyberturvallisuuden takia osa osa internetsivustoista rajoittaa niille pääsyä Nigeriasta, ja ilman VPN-yhteyttä tavanomaisimmillekin sivustoille pääsyssä voi olla esteitä. Nigerian kyberrikoslaki (päivitetty 2024) kriminalisoi erilaiset kyberrikokset laajasti.

themes.heading: Maahantulosäännökset
themes.text: UM ei vastaa vieraiden valtioiden maahantulosäännöksistä, kuten passin voimassaoloa koskevista ehdoista tai viisumivelvollisuudesta. Varmista ajantasaiset maahantulosäädökset kohdemaan viranomaisilta, esimerkiksi maan lähimmästä edustustosta. Lue lisää Suomen kansalaisen matkustusasiakirjoista osoitteessa https://um.fi/passi/

themes.heading: Abujan-suurlähetystö
themes.text: NO.9, Iro Dan Musa Street PLOT 1547, ASOKORO ABUJA NIGERIA +234 803 785 1150 sanomat.aba@gov.fi https://finlandabroad.fi/web/nga/frontpage https://www.facebook.com/embassyfinlandabuja https://www.instagram.com/finlandinnigeria/ https://linkedin.com/company/embassy-of-finland-in-nigeria Päivystys: Hätätapauksissa ota yhteys ulkoministeriön päivystykseen: puhelin +358 9 1605 5555 ja sähköposti paivystys.um@gov.fi.

themes.heading: Sicherheitsstufe
themes.text: country-security"> Stand 20.07.2026 (Unverändert gültig seit: 18.05.2026) Sicherheitsstufe Sicherheitsstufe 4 (regional) seit 31.01.2018 Landkarte Regionale Reisewarnung Sicherheitsstufe 4 (von 4) gilt in den Bundesstaaten Abia, Adamawa, Akwa Ibom, Anambra, Bauchi, Bayelsa, Benue, Borno, Cross Rivers, Delta, Ebonyi, Edo, Ekiti, Enugu, Gombe, Imo, Jigawa, Kaduna, Kano, Katsina, Kebbi, Kogi, Nassarawa, Niger, Ondo, Plateau, Rivers, Sokoto, Taraba, Yobe und Zamfara sowie die vorgelagerten Küsten Gewässer Golf von Guinea, Niger-Delta, Bucht von Benin und Bucht von Bonny. Hohes Sicherheitsrisiko Sicherheitsstufe 3 (von 4) für den Rest des Landes. Die Sicherheitslage kann sich jederzeit verändern und entwickeln. Daher empfehlen wir Ihnen: Nützen Sie unsere Auslandsservice-App sowie die kostenlose Reiseregistrierung! Informieren Sie sich über die Sicherheitslage vor Ort und überprüfen Sie diese regelmäßig!

themes.heading: Sicherheit & Kriminalität
themes.text: Regionale Reisewarnung (Sicherheitsstufe 4) Die Sicherheitssituation ist im gesamten Land angespannt und weiterhin unbeständig. Es kommt auf vielen Strecken auch untertags regelmäßig zu schweren Zwischenfällen (Überfälle, Entführungen mit Lösegeldforderungen sowie sonstige Gewaltverbrechen) – besonders gefährdet ist u.a. die Strecke Abuja–Kaduna. Fahrten in der Dunkelheit sin dzu vermeiden. Reisen sollten nach Möglichkeit mit dem Flugzeug erfolgen. Aktuelles: Im ganzen Land kommt es immer wieder zu teils gewalttätigen Protesten. Weitere Proteste können kurzfristig und ohne Vorankündigung auftreten. Die Botschaft ruft daher alle sich in Nigeria aufhaltenden Österreicherinnen und Österreicher zu erhöhter Vorsicht auf. Treffen Sie folgende allgemeine Vorkehrungen: verfolgen Sie die lokalen Medien aufmerksam; meiden Sie Menschenmengen, Versammlungen und Kundgebungen; seien Sie achtsam und beobachten Sie Ihre Umgebung stets aufmerksam; leisten Sie den Anweisungen der lokalen Behörden Folge; tragen Sie stets ein gültiges (idealerweise international anerkanntes) Reisedokument bei sich; halten Sie Ihr Mobiltelefon für den Notfall aufgeladen; legen Sie ausreichend Vorräte, v.a. Trinkwasser, haltbare Lebensmittel und Treibstoff, an. Allgemeines: Nigeria ist mit mehreren, sich überlagernden Sicherheitskrisen konfrontiert. Die staatlichen Sicherheitskräfte sind vielerorts überlastet, Reaktionszeiten sind lang, und eine verlässliche Gefahreneinschätzung ist häufig nicht möglich. In den nordöstlichen Landesteilen operieren islamistische Terrorgruppen - allen voran Boko Haram (JAS) und die Islamische Staatprovinz Westafrika (ISWAP) – die regelmäßig Anschläge verüben und zeitweise Kontrolle über einzelne Gebiete ausüben. Im Nordwesten des Landes, insbesondere in der Grenzregion zur Republik Niger kommt es regelmäßig zu Angriffen bewaffneter Gruppen, Entführungen sowie Aktivitäten terroristischer und krimineller Akteure (so genannten Banditen). Am stärksten betroffen sind die Bundesstaaten Zamfara, Kaduna, Katsina und Sokoto. Im Niger-Delta begehen bewaffnete Gruppen schwere Gewaltverbrechen. Zudem sind verschiedene ökoterroristisch motivierte Gruppierungen aktiv und verüben regelmäßig Anschläge auf Rohstoffförderanlagen und Energieinfrastruktur. Im Südosten des Landes operieren separatistische Bewegungen. Sie rufen regelmäßig zu Boykotten auf („sit-at-home order“), welche mit Gewalt durchgesetzt werden. Zudem sind Anschläge und Entführungen mit Lösegeldforderungen möglich. Im „Middle Belt" des Landes kommt es seit Jahren zu bewaffneten Auseinandersetzungen zwischen sesshaften Bauern und nomadischen Hirtenvölkern. Der Südwesten gilt traditionell als ruhigerer Landesteil, jedoch nehmen auch dort Gewaltkriminalität sowie Spannungen und Unruhen zwischen den Volksgruppen zu. Entführungen mit Lösegeldforderungen sind auch in dieser Region keine Seltenheit. Auch in der Hauptstadt Abuja und der Handelsmetropole Lagos hat sich die Sicherheitslage in den vergangenen Jahren verschlechtert. Einbrüche, Diebstähle und bewaffnete Raubüberfälle, teils mit Todesfolge, kommen häufig vor, zuletzt auch vermehrt in als vergleichsweise sicher geltenden Stadtteilen. Proteste und Anschläge sind möglich. Potenzielle Anschlagsziele sind Hotels, internationale Organisationen, Märkte, Einkaufszentren sowie staatliche und religiöse Einrichtungen. Bei Straßenblockaden tarnen sich Kriminelle mitunter als Polizei- oder Militärpersonal. Nicht aufschiebbare Reisen auf dem Landweg sollten nur mit ortskundigen und zuverlässigen, möglichst persönlich bekannten und einheimischen Personen durchgeführt werden, vorzugsweise im Konvoi. Für Reisen ins Landesinnere wird die Nutzung professioneller Sicherheitsdienste dringend empfohlen. Nigeria ist zudem Ausgangspunkt für Internet-Betrug insbesondere Vorauszahlungsbetrug, fingierte Erbschaften, angebliche Geldtransfers und „Love Scams“ (vorgetäuschte Liebesbeziehungen via Internet). Hinweis: Reisewarnungen werden im Regelfall nur in besonderen Krisensituationen ausgesprochen, wenn eine generelle Gefährdung für Leib und Leben besteht. Reisende, die sich in ein Gebiet mit Reisewarnung begeben, müssen sich der möglichen Konsequenzen bewusst sein: Konsularische Hilfeleistung für in Not geratene Österreicherinnen und Österreicher ist nur äußerst eingeschränkt möglich. Bei Reisen in Gebiete mit Reisewarnung können Versicherungen trotz eines aufrechten Versicherungsvertrages Ausschlussgründe geltend machen und sind damit leistungsfrei. Das Konsulargebührengesetz ermächtigt die Republik Österreich, die Kosten für allenfalls erforderliche Schutzmaßnahmen bzw. Hilfeleistung in bestimmten Situationen im Regressweg von Reisenden zurückzufordern! Notrufnummern: Allgemeine Notrufnummer : 112 Ambulanz, Feuerwehr und Polizei: 112 Polizei, Feuerwehr, Ambulanz (alternativ): 199 Straßennotfälle (FRSC): 122

themes.heading: Einreise & Ausreise
themes.text: Reiseregistrierung: Jeweils vor der Anreise als auch vor der Abreise muss eine „Landing“ und „Exit“ Card. Die Karten müssen ausgedruckt und beim Check-in bzw. an der Grenzkontrolle vorgelegt werden. Ein rein digitaler Nachweis wird nicht akzeptiert. Die Karten sind spätestens 72 Stunden vor Abflug zu beantragen. Die Beantragung erfolgt auf: https://lecard.immigration.gov.ng. Bitte beachten Sie, dass sich die Einreisebestimmungen jederzeit ändern können. Informieren Sie sich daher vor Reiseantritt bei den zuständigen nigerianischen Behörden, dem Nigerian Immigration Service oder der Botschaft der Bundesrepublik Nigeria in Wien. Visumpflicht: Ja Visum erhältlich: Für die Einreise nach Nigeria benötigen österreichische Staatsangehörige ein Visum. Der Antrag ist grundsätzlich über https://immigration.gov.ng/nigerian-visa/ zu stellen. Seit 1. Mai 2025 gilt das neue E-Visa-System des Nigerian Immigration Service (NIS). Das bisherige Visa-on-Arrival-System wurde damit abgelöst. Kurzaufenthalts-E-Visa (30–90 Tage) für 13 Kategorien – darunter Tourismus, Business und Familienbesuch – können vollständig online beantragt werden, ohne persönliche Vorsprache bei einer Auslandsvertretung. Gültige Aufenthaltsdauer: 30 bis 90 Tage je nach Kategorie. E-Visa sind nicht verlängerbar; für längere Aufenthalte ist vorab ein entsprechendes Visum über die nigerianische Auslandsvertretung zu beantragen. Für Visakategorien außerhalb der E-Visa-Kategorien (z.B. Arbeitsvisa, längere Aufenthalte) ist weiterhin eine persönliche Antragstellung bei der zuständigen nigerianischen Auslandsvertretung erforderlich. Antragsportal: https://evisa.immigration.gov.ng Technische Probleme beim Portal wurden nach dem Start im Mai 2025 gemeldet. Es wird empfohlen, die Beantragung rechtzeitig vor Abreise durchzuführen. Weitere Informationen finden Sie auf der Homepage der Botschaft der Bundesrepublik Nigeria in Wien. Reisedokumente: Reisepass Passgültigkeit: 6 Monate bei Einreise Cremefarbiger Notpass: Wird akzeptiert. Konventions- oder Fremdenpass: Wenden Sie sich bitte an die weiter unten angeführte „ausländische Vertretung in Österreich“. Sonstiges: Reisende müssen ihre Wiederausreise (Rückflug- oder Weiterreiseticket) nachweisen können. Eine Einladung einer in Nigeria wohnhaften Person oder einer Behörde ist je nach Visakategorie Voraussetzung für die Visumerteilung. Für E-Visa beträgt die Bearbeitungszeit mindestens 48 Stunden. Für Visa über die nigerianische Auslandsvertretung kann die Bearbeitung bis zu zehn Werktage oder länger dauern. Die bei Einreise tatsächlich erlaubte Aufenthaltsdauer kann auf maximal vier Wochen begrenzt werden. E-Visa sind ausdrücklich nicht verlängerbar. Für traditionelle Visa (über Auslandsvertretung beantragt) kann eine Verlängerung nach der Einreise beim Nigerian Immigration Service beantragt werden. Seit 1. September 2025 gelten neue Strafbestimmungen: USD 15 pro Tag der Überschreitung; ab 3 Monaten Überschreitung zusätzlich ein 5-jähriges Einreiseverbot; ab 1 Jahr Überschreitung permanentes Einreiseverbot. Vor dem Abflug muss online eine Health Declaration Form ausgefüllt werden (https://healthapp.ncdc.gov.ng ). Das Formular gilt für alle einreisenden Passagiere und erfasst Gesundheitszustand, Impfstatus und Reisehistorie. Eine ausgedruckte Kopie sollte mitgeführt werden; ein ausschließlich digitaler Nachweis kann zu Problemen bei der Einreise führen. Kann das Formular nicht vorab ausgefüllt werden, sind Papierversionen am Flughafen erhältlich. Für die Einreise nach Nigeria ist der Nachweis einer Gelbfieberimpfung durch Vorlage eines internationalen Impfpasses zu erbringen. Minderjährige: Für Minderjährige (bis 18 Jahre), die ohne Begleitung der gesetzlichen Vertreter oder mit nur einem gesetzlichen Vertreter verreisen, wird – zusätzlich zum eigenen Reisepass – auch eine Einverständniserklärung für die Reise benötigt. Dieser Vollmacht sollte eine Kopie der Geburtsurkunde des bzw. der Minderjährigen sowie eine Kopie der Reisepässe der gesetzlichen Vertreter angeschlossen sein. Bei unterschiedlichen Nachnamen empfiehlt sich auch die Mitnahme der Heiratsurkunde der Eltern. Muster für eine Einverständniserklärung finden Sie auf der Seite des ÖAMTC und des ARBÖ. Diebstahl/Verlust des Reisedokuments: Von der Verwendung gestohlener oder verlorener und wieder gefundener Reisedokumente wird abgeraten, auch wenn die Anzeige bei der zuständigen Behörde bereits widerrufen wurde, da Probleme an der Grenze bis zur Einreiseverweigerung nicht ausgeschlossen werden können.

themes.heading: Einfuhr & Ausfuhr
themes.text: Die Ein- und Ausfuhr von Fremdwährung über einem Gegenwert von 10.000 USD ist deklarationspflichtig. Die Deklaration muss online über das E-Currency-Declaration-Formular des Nigeria Customs Service erfolgen: https://cdf.nigeriatradehub.gov.ng. Nicht deklarierte Beträge über diesem Grenzwert können beschlagnahmt und strafrechtlich verfolgt werden. Es empfiehlt sich, ausreichend US-Dollar oder Naira in bar mitzuführen. Bargeld (USD und NGN) ist das am weitesten verbreitete Zahlungsmittel – auch in den Großstädten. Euro werden von Wechselstuben und manchen Hotels akzeptiert, sind jedoch kein standardisiertes Zahlungsmittel. Kreditkarten (Visa, Mastercard) werden in größeren Hotels, Einkaufszentren und Restaurants in Lagos und Abuja akzeptiert, jedoch sollten sie angesichts von Skimming-Risiken nur mit äußerster Vorsicht verwendet werden. Travellerschecks sind praktisch nicht mehr einlösbar und werden nicht empfohlen. Gegenstände für den persönlichen Bedarf können zollfrei eingeführt werden. Die Einfuhr von Getränken und Lebensmitteln, sowie Textilien ist verboten. Für die Einfuhr von Pflanzen, Samen und Blumen ist vorab eine Genehmigung der Plant Quarantine Service (Federal Dept. of Agricultural Research) erforderlich. Die Einfuhr von Schmuck und Edelmetallen ist verboten.Die Ausfuhr von Artefakten, Antiquitäten und Kulturgütern ist verboten bzw. nur mit schriftlicher Genehmigung des Department of Antiquities erlaubt. Ebenfalls verboten ist die Ausfuhr von Rohholz, unbearbeitetem Kautschuk, Schrott, Rohhäuten sowie von gefährdeten Tierarten und deren Produkten. Nähere Auskünfte finden Sie auch im Travel Centre der IATA. Die angeführten Mengen und Beträge sind unverbindliche Richtangaben, rechtsverbindliche Informationen kann nur die Vertretungsbehörde dieses Landes erteilen. Bitte beachten Sie bei der Einreise nach Österreich die geltenden Einfuhrbestimmungen.

themes.heading: Gesundheit
themes.text: Krankenhäuser bzw. die gesamte Gesundheitsversorgung entsprechen nicht dem europäischen Standard. In verschiedenen Landesteilen kommt es regelmäßig zu Ausbrüchen von Cholera und Typhus. Cholera ist in Nigeria endemisch; 2024 stiegen die Verdachtsfälle laut NCDC um rund 220 % an (über 10.000 Verdachtsfälle, 359 Todesfälle bis Herbst 2024). Auch Anfang 2025 wurden weiterhin Fälle in mehreren Bundesstaaten gemeldet. Auch Tollwut ist in Nigeria verbreitet. Einziger möglicher Schutz ist eine Impfung beziehungsweise die Vermeidung von Tierkontakten. Seit 2023 besteht ein anhaltender Diphtherie-Ausbruch in nahezu allen Bundesstaaten Nigerias. Eine aktuelle Diphtherie-Schutzimpfung (Tdap) wird vor Reiseantritt dringend empfohlen. Ein erhöhtes Gelbfiebergeschehen besteht seit dem Ausbruch im November 2020 weiterhin in mehreren Bundesstaaten (u.a. Bauchi, Benue, Delta, Ebonyi, Enugu). Die Impfung ist Einreisepflicht und sollte mindestens 10 Tage vor Abreise erfolgen. Nigeria gehört weiterhin zu den Ländern mit zirkulierendem Poliovirus. Eine Auffrischung des Polio-Impfschutzes wird vor der Reise empfohlen. Achten Sie auf Insektenschutz zur Vermeidung von Tropenkrankheiten! Es besteht im gesamten Land ganzjährig eine hohe Malaria-Ansteckungsgefahr, auch Lassa-Fieber ist in Nigeria endemisch. Besprechen Sie Vorkehrungsmaßnahmen mit einem Arzt und das korrekte Verhalten im Falle einer Infektion. Aufgrund diverser Parasiten (v.a. Bilharziose) wird vom Schwimmen in Süßwassergewässern abgeraten. Außerhalb der Städte gibt es nur sehr eingeschränkte oder gar keine medizinischen Versorgungsmöglichkeiten. Es kommt immer wieder zu Fällen von Handel mit gefälschten Medikamenten. Auf den Verzehr von rohen Speisen sollte verzichtet werden bzw. ist höchste Vorsicht geboten. Trinken Sie ausschließlich abgekochtes, gefiltertes oder in versiegelten Flaschen abgefülltes Wasser. Durchfallerkrankungen sind die häufigste Reiseerkrankung in Nigeria. Es besteht kein Sozialversicherungsabkommen mit Österreich. Es wird darauf hingewiesen, dass die Kosten einer allfälligen Krankenhausbehandlung erheblich sein können. Der Abschluss einer alle Risiken abdeckenden Reiseversicherung wird daher vor Reiseantritt dringend empfohlen. Die Mitnahme einer Reiseapotheke, die nicht nur regelmäßig benötigte Arzneimittel, sondern auch Medikamente für gängige Reiseerkrankungen beinhaltet, wird empfohlen. Wer auf bestimmte Medikamente angewiesen ist, sollte einen ausreichenden Vorrat und einen Nachweis über die ärztliche Verschreibung mitnehmen, auf der Homepage des Sozialministeriums finden Sie nähere Informationen zur Mitnahme von Medikamenten ins Ausland. Es wird empfohlen, 4–8 Wochen vor Reisebeginn eine reisemedizinische Beratung aufzusuchen (Hausarzt oder spezialisierte Reisemedizin-Einrichtung), um den individuellen Impfschutz zu überprüfen und erforderliche Impfungen rechtzeitig zu erhalten. Einige Impfstoffe erfordern mehrere Dosen über mehrere Wochen.

themes.heading: Infrastruktur
themes.text: Die Flugsicherheitsstandards variieren je nach Airline. Einzelne nigerianische Fluggesellschaften verfügen über eine IOSA-Zertifizierung die internationalen Standards entspricht. Dennoch ist bei Inlandsflügen grundsätzlich erhöhte Vorsicht angebracht. Auf die Benützung öffentlicher Verkehrsmittel, inkl. Zügen sollte verzichtet werden. Mieten Sie ausschließlich registrierte Limousinen-Dienstleister und Hotelwagen. Die Nutzung privater Fahrtendienste wie Uber, Bolt oder inDrive ist mit einem Risiko verbunden. Sicherheitsvorfälle – darunter Überfälle auf Passagiere und Fahrer – wurden gemeldet, insbesondere bei der Plattform inDrive, die weniger strenge Fahrerüberprüfungen durchführt. Bolt und Uber verfügen über GPS-Tracking und Fahrerverifikation. Empfohlen wird: Fahrtdetails mit einer Vertrauensperson teilen, Nachtfahrten vermeiden und nur Fahrzeuge besteigen, deren Kennzeichen mit der App-Anzeige übereinstimmt. Der Straßenverkehr ist landesweit mit erheblichen Risiken verbunden und insbesondere nachts äußerst gefährlich. Fahrzeuge sind häufig in schlechtem technischem Zustand und riskantes Fahrverhalten ist verbreitet. Die Straßeninfrastruktur ist in vielen Landesteilen mangelhaft; während der Regenzeit (April bis Oktober) sind zahlreiche Straßen, insbesondere im Landesinneren, nur eingeschränkt oder gar nicht passierbar. Nach Verkehrsunfällen ist nicht mit einer verlässlichen Erstversorgung oder Verfügbarkeit von Rettungsdiensten zu rechnen. In Einzelfällen kann es zudem zu aggressiven Reaktionen oder Vergeltungshandlungen durch umstehende bzw. beteiligte Personen kommen. Reisen auf dem Landweg sollten daher nur mit besonderer Vorsicht und nach Möglichkeit bei Tageslicht erfolgen. Teilweise kommt es weiterhin zu Engpässen in der Treibstoffversorgung. Bei Autofahrten sind neben dem heimischen Führerschein folgende Dokumente mitzuführen: Internationaler Führerschein (IDP), Fahrzeugzulassung, Kfz-Versicherungsnachweis sowie – bei Einreise mit eigenem Fahrzeug – ein Carnet de Passages en Douane (CPD). Seit Jänner 2026 erhalten internationale Reisende, die ihr eigenes Fahrzeug temporär nach Nigeria einführen, nach Vorlage dieser Dokumente eine befristete Einreisegenehmigung für das Fahrzeug (Temporary Vehicle Admission Permit, gültig bis zu 90 Tage). Eine zusätzliche Fahrerlaubnis der Polizei ist nicht erforderlich.. Vorsicht ist bei Fahrzeugkontrollen geboten, da diese von Straßenräubern in Polizeiuniformen durchgeführt werden könnten. Mehr Informationen zur Straßenverkehrsordnung finden Sie in der Länderdatenbank des ÖAMTC.

themes.heading: Umwelt
themes.text: Wechsel von Regen- und Trockenzeit, tropisches feuchtheißes Klima im Süden, im Norden eher trocken und sehr heiß. Im Süden dauert die Regenzeit von Mai bis Anfang November; trockener Wüstenwind (Harmattan) von Dezember bis Februar. In der Regenzeit (April bis Oktober) kommt es regelmäßig zu teils verheerenden Überschwemmungen, die große Zerstörung anrichten, Verkehrswege unterbrechen und zahlreiche Menschenleben fordern.

themes.heading: Besondere Bestimmungen
themes.text: Die Rechtslage kann sich in den einzelnen Bundesstaaten unterscheiden, unter anderem in Bezug auf Verkauf und Konsum von Alkohol. Erkundigen Sie sich daher bei ortsansässigen Bekannten oder im Hotel über die lokal gültigen Bestimmungen. Eine besondere Rücksichtnahme auf islamische Sitten und Gebräuche ist v.a. in den muslimisch geprägten nördlichen und den zentralen Bundesstaaten zu empfehlen. Gleichgeschlechtliche sexuelle Handlungen sind im gesamten Land strafbar: Im säkularen Bundesrecht drohen bis zu 14 Jahre Freiheitsstrafe. In den 12 nördlichen Bundesstaaten, die islamisches Scharia-Recht anwenden, kann für Männer die Todesstrafe durch Steinigung verhängt werden; für Frauen gilt Freiheitsstrafe oder Auspeitschung (in Kano und Katsina auch Todesstrafe). Das Schutzalter für sexuelle Handlungen beträgt nach dem bundesweit anwendbaren Child Rights Act (2003) 18 Jahre. Dieser Akt wurde jedoch erst von 31 der 36 Bundesstaaten ratifiziert; in den übrigen Staaten (v.a. nördliche Bundesstaaten) können abweichende, teils niedrigere Altersgrenzen nach dem Strafgesetzbuch (Criminal Code Act: 16 Jahre) oder lokalen Bestimmungen gelten. Sexuelle Handlungen mit Minderjährigen sind grundsätzlich strafbar.

themes.heading: Latest update
themes.text: Still current at:19 July 2026 Updated:15 July 2026 Latest update:We continue to advise reconsider your need to travel to Nigeria due to the threat of terrorism, kidnapping, violent crime and the risk of civil unrest. We advise do not travel to a number of states in Nigeria. There's a high risk of terrorist attacks across Nigeria. Terrorist attacks may be more likely around religious holidays and other significant dates. Always be alert to threats, especially in public places. Avoid public demonstrations, rallies and gatherings. These can occur at short notice and may turn violent (see 'Safety'). Nigerian authorities have introduced strict entry requirements in response to the Ebola disease outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda. If you travel to Nigeria, you'll need to complete a Health Declaration Form online before you travel or on arrival. You may also be subject to additional health screening measures on arrival, such as travel history and body temperature checks (see 'Travel'). We advise: Reconsider your need to travel to Nigeria overall due to the volatile security situation and threat of terrorism, kidnapping, violent crime and the risk of civil unrest. Higher levels apply in some areas. Download Nigeria Download Africa

themes.heading: Safety
themes.text: There's a high risk of terrorist attacks across Nigeria by various militant groups. Attacks could be indiscriminate or may target foreign interests. Terrorist attacks may be more likely around religious holidays and other significant dates. Potential targets include places where crowds gather, such as government buildings, law enforcement and military facilities, hotels, bars, restaurants, political meetings, places of worship, schools, markets and shopping malls, sporting events, transport hubs and networks, international organisations and camps for displaced people. Avoid all demonstrations, strikes, rallies and large gatherings. These can turn violent and have sometimes been met with forceful suppression by security forces, including the use of live fire. Security measures around western facilities, such as US embassies and western-affiliated schools may disrupt travel, businesses and services, particularly in Abuja and Lagos. The kidnapping threat is high throughout Nigeria. Foreign nationals, including Australians, have been kidnapped in the past. Businesspeople, workers in extractives industries, tourists, journalists and humanitarian aid workers are seen as legitimate targets. You should organise professional security advice and support before travelling to Nigeria. There's a high rate of crime in Nigeria, including violent crime. The homicide rate is very high in many parts of the country. Follow the instructions of local authorities. Avoid intercity transport by road and rail due to the high incidence of criminal and terrorist attacks. Australian officials always use enhanced security measures when they travel. Keep your car doors locked, windows up and valuables out of sight. Arrange airport transfers with someone you know and trust, or book a hotel car. Don't use ridesharing services, taxis, or public transport. Don't travel after dark. Internet romance, friendship and employment scams originate from Nigeria. Be wary of online contact from people you don't know. Don't send money until you've checked who you're sending it to. If you're a scam victim, you should not travel to Nigeria to try to get your money back or seek justice, or you may face physical harm or robbery. Be alert to the potential risks around drink spiking and methanol poisoning through consuming alcoholic drinks. See our advice on partying safely while overseas. Full travel advice: Safety

themes.heading: Health
themes.text: Yellow fever is present. Other insect-borne diseases include malaria and dengue. Ensure your accommodation is insect-proof. Use insect repellent. Consider taking anti-malarial medication. Check your vaccinations are up to date before you travel. Poliovirus (Vaccine Derived Poliovirus Type 2) outbreaks have occurred in Nigeria. Consider a booster if it has been 10 years since your last polio vaccine. Foodborne, waterborne and infectious diseases found in Nigeria include cholera, typhoid, hepatitis, diphtheria, mpox, bilharzia and Lassa fever. Make sure food and drink utensils and containers are clean before use. Drink only bottled water and avoid raw or undercooked food. Don't swim in fresh water. Medical facilities throughout Nigeria are often basic. You may need to pay up-front. If you're seriously ill or injured, you'll need medical evacuation. Ensure your travel insurance covers this. Full travel advice: Health

themes.heading: Local laws
themes.text: Don't use or carry illegal drugs. Penalties for drug offences include long prison sentences and large fines. Serious crimes, such as murder and armed robbery, can attract the death penalty. Nigeria has strict laws against same-sex relationships. Avoid public displays of affection and adhere to conservative dress codes. In Sharia law states in northern Nigeria, there are harsh punishments for adultery, same-sex relationships or public expression deemed blasphemous or religiously disrespectful. Extra-judicial executions of LGBTQIA+ people have been carried out in the past. Avoid taking photographs of military or police locations or personnel, or in airports. Smoking in public places is prohibited. Be aware of Nigerian restrictions on the import or export of foreign currencies, art, artefacts and antiques, precious metals, foods, drinks and other items. Further information is available from the Nigerian Customs service. Full travel advice: Local laws

themes.heading: Travel
themes.text: You'll need a visa to travel to Nigeria. You can apply and pay for a visa online on the Nigeria Immigration Service website. Complete landing and departure cards online before arriving and departing the country. Entry and exit conditions can change at short notice. Contact the nearest embassy, high commission or consulate of Nigeria for details about visas, customs and quarantine rules. You'll need a valid yellow fever vaccination certificate (yellow book) to enter. You’ll also need to complete a Health Declaration Form prior to entering Nigeria. You may be subject to additional health screening measures on arrival, such as travel history and body temperature checks. Hijacking, kidnapping and armed robbery at sea occurs in Nigerian waters, especially in the Niger Delta region. Piracy is a threat in the Gulf of Guinea.

themes.heading: Local contacts
themes.text: There are limits to what consular assistance we can provide in high-risk locations. Read the Consular Services Charter to understand how we can assist you overseas. To stay up to date with local information, follow the High Commission’s social media accounts. The Australian High Commission in Abuja and Australia’s Honorary Consulate in Lagos provide limited consular and passport services. If you travel to 'do not travel' areas despite our advice, we may not be able to provide you with consular services. Full travel advice: Local contacts

themes.heading: Security situation
themes.text: We continue to advise reconsider your need to travel due to the threat of terrorism, kidnapping, violent crime and the risk of civil unrest. Higher levels apply in some areas. We also advise do not travel to Kwara State, in addition to a number of other 'do not travel' states in Nigeria. If you travel to Nigeria, you're at risk of: terrorist attack kidnapping civil unrest crime The security situation could change suddenly. You could get caught up in violence directed at others. If you travel to high-risk areas despite our advice, it is unlikely we will be able to provide you consular services. Seek professional security advice and arrange personal security measures before travelling to Nigeria. Violent crime is common in Nigeria, including in parts of Lagos and Abuja. Politically-motivated violence, including terrorist bombings and armed attacks, occurs frequently. Police maintain patrols and checkpoints in many locations in Nigeria, including at entry points into, and key junctions within cities. Police officers, or people posing as police officers, may ask for bribes. It's illegal to pay bribes to foreign officials. Intimidation may be used, including a display of weapons. We strongly recommend you don’t drive at night (see 'Travel'). For more information on the situation in Nigeria: US State Department travel advice for Nigeria US Embassy in Nigeria Security Alerts UK Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office travel advice for Nigeria Global Affairs Canada Travel Advice for Nigeria

themes.heading: Terrorism
themes.text: There's a high risk of terrorist attacks in Nigeria by various militant groups. Further attacks are highly likely. If you're in a 'do not travel' area, leave if it's safe to do so. Terrorist attacks: can happen anywhere and at any time may specifically target religious and public holidays may be indiscriminate may target venues frequently visited by foreign nationals Potential targets may include: hotels, bars, restaurants, markets and shopping malls political meetings and government buildings law enforcement and military facilities religious gatherings and places of worship schools and universities international organisations sports, concerts and other events transport hubs (including airports, bus and train networks) camps for displaced people other places where crowds gather Terrorists may seek to target the staff of, or locations closely associated with, foreign governments. Australian officials rarely travel outside of Abuja and Lagos, due to the high-risk security environment. Australian officials always use enhanced security measures when they travel. Attacks can be large, simultaneous, and might include: explosives, including suicide bombers knives, machetes or other sharp-edged weapons gunfire kidnapping Boko Haram and the Islamic State West Africa Province are active across Nigeria. These groups and others are most active north of the Federal Capital Territory, especially in the north-eastern states, including Adamawa, Bauchi, Borno, Gombe and Yobe. However, they have shown an intent and capability to launch attacks further afield, including Abuja. Armed criminal bandit groups conduct attacks on civilians, motorists and local communities throughout Nigeria. Armed groups often target transport routes. Airports could be targeted. Avoid intercity transport by road and rail. If despite our advice you undertake travel within Nigeria, research routes and get professional security advice and support before travelling. To protect yourself from terrorism: always be alert to threats, especially in public places avoid non-essential travel avoid crowds, and if you must go to a crowded place, have a clear exit plan monitor the media for threats, curfews and other restrictions take official warnings seriously follow the advice of local news and security authorities. If there’s a terrorist incident, leave the area if it’s safe to do so and follow the instructions of local authorities. Avoid the affected area in case of secondary attacks. More information: Terrorism

themes.heading: Kidnapping
themes.text: Kidnapping occurs across the world with political, ideological, and criminal motives. Foreign nationals, including Australians, have been kidnapped overseas whilst travelling. Kidnappings can happen anywhere, anytime. Victims may be held for extended periods, and in some cases executed. The threat of kidnapping is high in all parts of Nigeria, including in Lagos and Abuja. Terrorist organisations and criminal groups engage in kidnapping throughout Nigeria. Seaborne kidnapping in the Niger Delta and Gulf of Guinea regions is also a significant risk. Kidnappers usually target locals or Nigerian dual-nationals, but foreign nationals have been kidnapped in the past, including Australians. Businesspeople, workers in extractives industries, tourists, journalists and humanitarian aid workers are seen as legitimate targets. If despite our advice, you travel to an area with a high risk of kidnapping, our ability to provide assistance in these destinations is extremely limited. You’re responsible for your own safety. You can find more information on how to reduce your risk of kidnapping here: Kidnapping The Australian Government's longstanding policy is that it doesn't make payments or concessions to kidnappers. Ransom payments to kidnappers have funded further terrorist attacks and criminal activity. Ransom payments to terrorist groups are likely to contravene Australian counter-terrorism financing laws. More information: Scams that affect travellers Reducing the risk of piracy

themes.heading: Civil unrest and political tension
themes.text: The risk of election-related violence in Nigeria is high. Politically motivated murders and kidnappings have occurred. Clashes between political supporters and their opponents or government authorities are common. Foreign nationals are unlikely to be specifically targeted by election-related violence or civil unrest. However, you could get caught up in election-related events and violence directed at others. Demonstrations, protests and inter-communal violence can occur at short notice and may turn violent. They have sometimes been subject to forceful suppression by government security forces, including the use of tear gas, rubber bullets and live fire. Government authorities may also impose local curfews, states of emergency or suspend communications networks with little warning. Long-standing tribal, ethnic, religious, political and community arguments can lead to violence and unrest. Recent civil unrest has included conflict between cattle herders and villagers in many rural locations of Nigeria competing over access to land and resources. In urban settings there may be clashes between rival gangs known colloquially as ‘Area Boys’. We continue to advise against all travel to Rivers state. If despite our advice you travel there, comply with all directions from authorities and monitor local media reporting for updates on the situation. Security forces have maintained a heightened presence in Rivers state, resulting in restrictions on movement, searches and seizures, and questioning and detainment of persons. There have been strikes and protests over the cost of living in Nigeria in recent years. These could resume with little warning. Trading and business disruptions have occurred as a result of strike and protest action. You should maintain stocks of essential supplies, such as food, water and petrol in anticipation of periods of unrest. To protect yourself if you encounter a protest or local tension: leave and avoid the immediate affected area monitor local media and other sources for updates plan for interruptions to phone and internet services follow the instructions of local authorities. More information: Demonstrations and civil unrest

themes.heading: Crime
themes.text: Nigeria's crime rate is high. Crime increases at night, particularly in major cities and on highways. Criminals have targeted foreign nationals, as their perceived wealth makes them attractive targets. The following forms of crime are common: violent and sexual assault armed robbery and banditry home invasion or burglary carjacking and kidnapping (see 'Kidnapping') assaults and robberies on public transport or in taxis attacks on motorists travelling between cities (see ‘Road travel’). If you're a victim of violent crime, especially rape, seek immediate medical attention. Nigeria has a high rate of HIV/AIDS infections. To protect yourself from crime: avoid travel alone or after dark avoid conspicuous displays of money, devices including mobile phones, smartwatches or laptops, jewellery or expensive looking watches, in public places or in the presence of persons unknown to you take care when changing money or using ATMs secure valuable personal items when leaving hotel rooms arrange airport and hotel pick-ups using the airport transfer arrangements of a major hotel. If you don't know the driver, make sure you check their credentials before you leave the airport use hotel car services rather than local taxis or rideshare apps.

themes.heading: Scams and fraud
themes.text: Commercial and internet fraud often originates in Nigeria. ATM, debit and credit card fraud is also common (see 'Money'). Criminals often seek details of 'safe' bank accounts overseas, such as yours. They claim they will transfer large sums of money into the account. Victims believe they will get a percentage of the amount involved. Be aware of other scams where criminals: give fake cheques for 'urgent' shipments of goods ask for large fees for fake government contracts pretend to be calling from your financial institution extort money from people the scammers convince to travel to Nigeria for a business opportunity promise IVF or fertility treatments that involve staged births or ‘miracle babies’. To protect yourself against a scam, be wary of: any request to send money to a person in Nigeria, including people you know any invitation to Nigeria to meet someone you have met over the internet. Be careful about giving out personal information or disposing of personal documents while in Nigeria. Scammers can access your personal data when you use open networks. Be careful when and where you use internet banking and other sites that transmit or store your data or passwords. If you're travelling to Nigeria, your family and friends may receive bogus phone calls and emails from Nigeria. Scammers may claim that you're in legal, financial or medical trouble and that you need money. Some scammers may pretend to work for the Australian High Commission in Abuja and ask you to transfer money to the High Commission. Check with the High Commission first through its official contact details if you're asked to send money or information to them (see 'Local contacts'). Don't travel to Nigeria to try to get your money back or to seek justice or revenge. You could be in danger.

themes.heading: Extreme weather and natural disasters
themes.text: Nigeria experiences a rainy season from May to October. In past years, there have been significant casualties and widespread damage to infrastructure caused by severe flooding. Essential services may be disrupted. Follow the advice of local authorities and check the media for updates. The dry season is from November to April. Temperatures may be higher than 40°C for multiple days, especially in the north. Nigeria experiences a seasonal wind called the Harmattan from December to March blowing sand and dust. Dust storms are common and can reduce air quality and may affect people with respiratory conditions. The Harmattan can reduce visibility, creating hazardous conditions for driving. Flights can also be affected due to poor visibility. Strong ocean currents occur along the coast. Conditions can change quickly and be challenging, even for strong swimmers. Beaches are generally unsupervised and have no warning systems in place for unsafe conditions. Many people drown each year. If extreme weather or a natural disaster happens, follow the advice of local authorities. More information: Global Disaster Alert and Coordination System National Emergency Management Agency

themes.heading: Travel insurance
themes.text: You need special travel insurance if you’re going to a high-risk destination, which can be expensive. We can’t guarantee your safety and security in another country or pay for your medical expenses, including evacuation. If you don’t have insurance, you shouldn’t travel. Get comprehensive travel insurance before you leave and closely read the Product Disclosure Statement (PDS) for what is and isn’t covered. It must cover all medical costs overseas, including evacuation. The Australian Government will not pay for these costs. If you can’t afford travel insurance, you shouldn’t travel. This is true for everyone, even if you are healthy and fit. If you don’t have insurance, you might have to pay thousands of dollars up front for medical care. Before you travel, check your policy, make sure: you know what activities and medical care your insurance covers, including evacuations your insurance covers you for your whole trip, including any stopovers you know any exclusions – for example, you might need to pay extra for activities like motorcycling, snow sports, scuba diving or bungee jumping. More information: Travel insurance

themes.heading: Physical and mental health
themes.text: Travelling overseas can be stressful and uncertain. You may experience unfamiliar environments, travel disruptions, changes in normal routines and limited access to health care. Not taking your prescribed medications is a common cause of mental ill-health for Australians overseas. If you are concerned about your welfare, or someone else's, contact your nearest Australian embassy, high commission, or consulate. You can also call the 24-hour Consular Emergency Centre on +61 2 6261 3305. Mental health resources: Information and advice on mental illness (HealthDirect). Living with mental illness (BeyondBlue) Medicare Mental Health (the Department of Health and Aged Care) More information: General health advice Mental health support overseas

themes.heading: HIV/AIDS
themes.text: HIV/AIDS is common in Nigeria. Take precautions if you engage in activities that may expose you to the virus.

themes.heading: Insect-borne diseases
themes.text: Yellow fever is common in Nigeria. Yellow fever is a potentially fatal virus spread by mosquitoes. It's prevented by vaccination, which is a requirement for entry into Nigeria. You must provide proof of vaccination in the form of a 'yellow book'. Get vaccinated before you leave. Other common insect-borne diseases include: malaria dengue To protect yourself from disease: make sure your accommodation is insect-proof use insect repellent wear long, loose, light-coloured clothes consider taking medicine to prevent malaria. Get medical advice if you have a fever, muscle pain, rash or severe headache.

themes.heading: Other health risks
themes.text: Lassa fever occurs in several states in Nigeria. Monitor local media and the World Health Organization for updates. Lassa fever is spread through contact with rodents or bodily fluids of infected people. To protect yourself: avoid contact with rodents and their droppings keep food in clean, rodent-proof containers don't eat any food if it could be contaminated avoid contact with the blood and body fluids of infected people avoid contact with items that may have come in contact with an infected person's blood or body fluids (clothes, bedding, needles, medical equipment). Other infectious diseases: Common waterborne, foodborne and other infectious diseases include: cholera typhoid hepatitis mpox bilharzia (schistosomiasis) tuberculosis rabies measles meningitis diphtheria Serious outbreaks occur from time to time. To protect yourself from illness: drink boiled water or bottled water with sealed lids avoid ice cubes avoid raw and undercooked food or food that may have been washed in untreated water, including salads don't swim in fresh water. Get medical advice if you have a fever or diarrhoea. Ask your doctor about health risks and vaccines before you go. More information: Infectious diseases

themes.heading: Medical care
themes.text: There’s no agreement between Australia and Nigeria for free healthcare. Tourists must pay for all medical care, even if they need to stay in hospital or be evacuated. This can be very expensive. Make sure your travel insurance covers you. The Australian Government won't pay for these costs. Medical facilities throughout Nigeria can be basic. Private hospitals in major cities are expensive. Expect to pay cash up-front before doctors, ambulances and hospitals will treat you, even in an emergency. There is no national ambulance service. Some hospitals own and operate their own services. Ambulance service response times are unreliable and may not be available outside of major cities. If you're seriously ill or injured, you'll need evacuation to a place with better facilities. Make sure you get travel insurance that covers hospital stays and medical evacuation, as these can be very expensive.

themes.heading: Drugs
themes.text: Penalties for drug offences are severe. They may include long jail terms and large fines. More information: Carrying or using drugs

themes.heading: Death penalty
themes.text: The death penalty can apply for serious crimes like murder and armed robbery in Nigeria.

themes.heading: LGBTQIA+ laws
themes.text: Same-sex relationships are illegal in Nigeria and largely regarded as socially unacceptable. It's illegal to: enter into a same-sex marriage or civil union plan or take part in a same-sex marriage ceremony register, operate or visit gay clubs, societies or organisations make any public show of a same-sex relationship wear clothing that does not conform to local gender-based dress norms Same-sex sexual activity between adults can carry severe penalties in states operating under Sharia law. LGBTQIA+ persons are generally discriminated against in Nigeria and have been subject to harassment, verbal and physical abuse, across the country. Extra-judicial executions of LGBTQIA+ persons have been carried out in the past. Avoid public displays of affection and adhere to conservative dress codes. More information: Advice for LGBTQIA+ travellers

themes.heading: Dual nationality
themes.text: While dual nationality is recognised in Nigeria, it may impact our ability to offer you consular services. If you're an Australian-Nigerian dual national and aged between 18 and 30, you may be required to join the National Youth Service Corps program. Nigerian authorities may insist dual nationals enter and exit on a Nigerian passport. Contact an embassy or consulate of Nigeria for advice before you travel. More information: Advice for dual nationals

themes.heading: Local customs
themes.text: Nigeria is ethnically and religiously diverse. Avoid behaving or dressing in ways that might offend conservative local standards or religious sensitivities, especially outside of urban areas and for women travelling alone. Respect religious and cultural customs and laws. The Islamic holy month of Ramadan is observed in Nigeria, especially in northern Nigeria. During Ramadan, eating, drinking and smoking may be illegal in public during the day (see 'Sharia Law'). If you're not fasting, avoid these activities around people who are. Seek local advice to avoid offence.

themes.heading: Visas and border measures
themes.text: Each country or territory decides its own entry and exit rules, including health border screening measures. To find out what you need to enter a destination, ask their embassy, consulate or immigration office.

themes.heading: Visas
themes.text: You'll need a valid visa or Nigerian residence permit to travel to or reside in Nigeria. You can check visa conditions and apply and pay for a visa online through the Nigeria Immigration Service website. To complete the process, you may need to provide your passport by mail or in person to the appropriate Nigerian diplomatic mission in your country. Significant penalties, including fines and detention, may apply for people who overstay their visa terms. Entry and exit conditions can change at short notice. Contact the nearest embassy, high commission or consulate of Nigeria for details about visas, customs and quarantine rules.

themes.heading: Border measures
themes.text: Expect enhanced security and health screening measures at airports. Foreign nationals must complete landing and departure cards before arriving and departing. You may be asked to show these at airports, including prior to boarding your flight. Nigerian authorities have introduced enhanced public health measures at international entry points in response to the current Ebola disease outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda. You're required to complete an online Health Declaration Form from the Nigeria Centre for Disease Control (NCDC) portal before boarding your flight. If you're unable to complete the form in advance of travel, you'll need to complete the form on arrival. You may also be subject to additional health screening measures on arrival, such as travel history and body temperature checks. More information: Nigeria Centre for Disease Control and Prevention

themes.heading: Yellow fever vaccination
themes.text: You'll need a valid yellow fever vaccination certificate to enter Nigeria. Some airlines may want to see your vaccination certificate prior to boarding your flight to Nigeria. Find out about returning to Australia after exposure to yellow fever. More information: Countries with a risk of yellow fever

themes.heading: Lost or stolen passport
themes.text: Your passport is valuable and can be used by others to commit crimes. Don’t let anyone trick you into giving it to them. Always keep it in a safe place. If your passport is lost or stolen, tell the Australian Government: In Australia: contact the Australian Passport Information Service. Overseas: contact the nearest Australian embassy or consulate.

themes.heading: Passport with ‘X’ gender identifier
themes.text: Australian passports comply with international standards for sex and gender. If your passport has an 'X' in the sex field, we can’t guarantee your entry or transit to other countries. Ask the embassy or consulate of your destinations if they’ll accept your passport with an 'X' gender marker. Check all transit destinations too. More information: LGBTQIA+ travellers

themes.heading: Money
themes.text: The local currency is the Nigerian Naira (NGN). Nigeria is still largely a cash-based economy. Cash shortages have occurred in the past. You must declare amounts of currencies, negotiable instruments, or precious stones/metals in excess of US$10,000 or its equivalent brought into or out of Nigeria. Facilities for changing traveller's cheques and Australian dollars are very limited. US dollars, British Pounds and Euros are easily exchangeable in Nigeria, and usually accepted at: major hotels banks foreign exchange services. ATMs are widely available in major cities only. However, non-Nigerian debit and credit cards are rarely accepted outside of major hotels. Most transactions need to be conducted using local cards, bank transfers or cash. ATMs usually dispense only a small amount of cash per transaction. Fraud and skimming are high risks when using debit and credit cards with ATMs or EFTPOS machines. Pay careful attention when using these or when others are handling your cards. Report any unauthorised transactions to your bank immediately.

themes.heading: Land borders
themes.text: Avoid travelling over land borders to and from Nigeria. Most destinations bordering Nigeria also have a high travel advice level, including do not travel to Niger and Chad. If you travel overland despite our advice, read the travel advice of the destination you're entering or exiting. Nigeria's border crossings can be unsafe and may require lengthy additional checks. Land borders may open and close at short notice. Check with local authorities if the specific border you propose to travel through is open at the time of travel.

themes.heading: Driving permit
themes.text: International Driving Permits are recognised in Nigeria for up to 3 months, provided they are accompanied by a valid driver's licence. After this period, you must obtain a local driver's licence.

themes.heading: Road travel
themes.text: Driving in Nigeria is dangerous due to: poorly maintained roads and vehicles poor local driving habits unpredictable pedestrians, including street vendors and beggars poor signage lack of working traffic lights poor road lighting high number of carjackings Be alert to security threats, particularly in traffic jams and at traffic lights. Keep your doors locked and windows up at all times. Avoid travel by domestic road transport between urban centres due to the high incidence of attacks on motorists by criminal gangs, bandits and terrorists. They may attempt to stop travellers using nails and branches on the road, by seeking assistance for fake breakdowns and injured people, and by setting up fake checkpoints. If you decide to travel via domestic road transport despite our advice, you should engage professional security for support and planning. Travelling in an armoured vehicle with a trusted security provider is the safest domestic road transport option. Expect delays when you travel. This can include: authorised and unauthorised police and military roadblocks checkpoints traffic diversions parking restrictions fuel shortages and queues These happen both in major cities and rural areas, including after dark. If you can't produce your identity documents, car registration or ownership papers, police may fine you or ask for a bribe. It's illegal to pay bribes to foreign officials. If you're in a traffic accident, nearby crowds may gather quickly. They may also overreact if they think you're at fault or use this as an opportunity to assault you or steal from you. Rental cars may be available but should be avoided as their maintenance record and insurance coverage cannot be assured. More information: Driving or riding

themes.heading: Taxis and public transport
themes.text: We recommend using car hire services that include a driver, organised through a major hotel or reputable security provider. Don’t use local taxis and app-based transport services due to poor safety standards and the risk of crime. This includes motorcycle and tricycle (Tuk-Tuk style) taxis called ‘Okada’ and ‘Keke’ respectively. Don't use public transport including bus and rail services, to travel within or between cities. Public transport is often unsafe, due to: unqualified and uninsured drivers poor vehicle maintenance high speed driving overcrowding being targeted by criminal gangs, bandits and terrorists Travelling in an armoured motor vehicle with professional security support is the safest option.

themes.heading: Sea travel
themes.text: At sea or on ships in port, you may encounter: piracy hijacking kidnapping armed robbery Do not travel in the Gulf of Guinea or coastal areas around the Niger Delta. Criminals with speedboats and high-calibre weapons commonly target pleasure craft, cruise liners, oil tankers, container ships and other large vessels in these areas. The Nigerian Navy has limited capacity to respond to piracy incidents. The International Maritime Bureau issues piracy reports. Safety and reliability of some domestic and inter-country ferry services is a concern. Maintenance procedures and safety standards are not to Australian standards. If you travel on these services, insurance may not cover you.

themes.heading: Air travel
themes.text: DFAT doesn’t give advice about how safe different airlines or flight paths are. Safety risks and weather events may cause flight delays and cancellations. Contact your travel agent or airline to confirm your plans. Airlines and airports can restrict what you can take in your luggage. Regulations may be different to those in Australia. Check with your airline before you travel. Domestic and international flights are often overbooked, delayed or cancelled with little notice. Safety and reliability of domestic airlines cannot be guaranteed. Airlines may not have strict maintenance procedures and safety standards. If you travel on certain airlines, you may not be covered by insurance. Check with your insurance provider before booking flights. Check Nigeria's air safety profile with the Aviation Safety Network. More information: Air travel

themes.heading: Medical emergencies
themes.text: Call 119, 112 or go to the nearest hospital. There is no national ambulance service. Some hospitals own and operate their own services. Ambulance service response times are unreliable and may not be available outside of major cities.

themes.heading: Consular contacts
themes.text: There are limits to what consular assistance we can provide in high-risk locations. Read the Consular Services Charter to understand how we can assist you overseas. For consular assistance, contact the Australian High Commission in Abuja. Public access to the Australian High Commission is strictly by appointment only.

themes.heading: Australian Honorary Consulate, Lagos
themes.text: 8th Floor, Union Marble House 1 Alfred Rewane Road (formerly Kingsway), Falomo Ikoyi Lagos, Nigeria Phone: (+234 0) 803 300 7291 Email: alandavies@jamescubittgroup.com The Honorary Consulate in Lagos can provide limited consular assistance and doesn’t issue Australian passports. You should first contact the Australian High Commission in Abuja for consular enquiries.

themes.heading: 24-hour Consular Emergency Centre
themes.text: In a consular emergency, if you can't contact an embassy, call the 24-hour Consular Emergency Centre on: +61 2 6261 3305 from overseas 1300 555 135 in Australia

themes.heading: 여행경보 (reiswaarschuwing)
themes.text:
여행자제: 주(에보니,에키티,에누구,라고스,오군,온도,오순,오요,베누에)
출국권고: 2단계를 제외한 전지역

themes.text:

themes.heading: 주재국 신고
themes.text: ㅇ 경찰 / 소방 / 웅급구호 긴급연락처 112 ㅇ 지방의 경우 열악한 통신사정으로 긴급전화가 불통될 수 있으므로, 지방정부 단위 긴급연락처(휴대전화 번호)를 사전 확인하는 것이 필요합니다.

themes.heading: 의료기관 연락처
themes.text: ㅇ 나이지리아의 경우 의료시설이 매우 열악하나 아래 병원에서 기본적인 응급조치는 가능합니다. [아부자] ㅇ Nizamiye Hospital : 0816-666-6023 ㅇ Dr Hassan's clinic : 0706-686-2500, 0810-368-5559 [라고스] ㅇ Reddington Hospital(Victoria Island) : 0812-800-8187, 01-271-5340(앰뷸런스 이용 가능) ㅇ St. Francis Hospital : 0907-972-0888, 0803-705-8454 ㅇ St. Nicholas Hospital : 0802-290-8484, 01-271-8690 ※ 말라리아 예방약 등 가정상비약은 약국에서 구매할 수 있으며, 약국은 주요 시설 및 호텔 내에서 찾으실 수 있습니다.

themes.heading: 기본정보 (basisinformatie)
themes.text: ㅇ 위치 : 북위 4°~14°, 동경 3°~14° ㅇ 수도 : 아부자 ㅇ 면적 : 923.768㎡(세계 32위, 한반도 면적의 약 4.25배) ㅇ 언어 : 영어(공용어), 하우사어, 플라니어, 요루바어, 이보어, 이조어 등 약 250여 토착 언어 ㅇ 종교 : 이슬람교(45%), 기독교(35%), 토착종교

themes.heading: 사건ㆍ사고의 유형
themes.text: ㅇ나이지리아 북동부지역(Borno, Adamawa, Yobe주 등)에는 이슬람극단주의 무장세력 Boko Haram과 ISWAP이 군경에 대한 직접 공격 및 민간인을 대상으로 한 납치살해 등 대규모 테러를 계속 자행하고 있습니다. ㅇ 중부지역에는 Fulani족 무슬림 유목민과 기독교인 농민간 무장 충돌로 지속 대규모 사망자가 발생하고 있습니다. ㅇ 남부지역에는 과거 Biafra 내전 이후 유전지대 독립을 주장하는 IPOB 등 분리주의단체가 자체 무장세력(ESN) 형성 등 정부와 갈등이 고조되고 있습니다. ㅇ 기니만 해역에서는 나이지리아 해적이 선박 해상강도 및 선원납치를 지속하고 있고, 전세계에서 가장 위험한 해역으로 평가되고 있습니다. - 2020년 우리 국민 피랍사건 3건 발생 ㅇ 나이지리아 전역의 도시 및 주요 도로에서 납치사건이 급증하고 있으며, 수십~수백명 단위의 대규모 납치도 계속 발생하고 있으며, 몸값을 지불하지 못하는 경우 생명을 잃는 사례도 증가하고 있습니다. ㅇ 도시 및 지방 이동 시 반드시 이동경로의 위험성에 대해 사전 확인하고, 개인신변 안전조치를 강구해야 합니다.

themes.heading: 자연재해
themes.text: ㅇ 우기철(5~10월)에는 중남부지역(Adamawa, Taraba, Plateau, Benue, Rivers, Bayelsa주 등)에서 대형 홍수가 발생, 수백만명의 수재민 피해 발생 사례가 잦습니다. ㅇ 순간적인 집중 폭우로 인한 도로 및 시설물 유실 사례가 많습니다. 특히, 우기철 여행 시 도로 유실 등 홍수 피해에 각별히 대비하셔야 합니다.

themes.heading: 유의해야 할 지역
themes.text: ㅇ 도시간 주요 도로에서도 피랍사건이 지속 발생하고 있어 육로를 이용한 도시간 이동 자제가 바람직합니다. ㅇ 수도 Abuja 및 경제도시 Lagos에서도 금품 목적의 강도 살인, 강도, 납치 사건이 항상 발생하고 있어, 외출 시 전 지역에서 항상 유의하셔야 합니다.

themes.heading: 출입국 (in- en uitreis)
themes.text: 무비자 입국 가능 여부(1회 입국시 최대 연속 체류 가능 일자) ㅇ 외교관 여권 소지자 : X ㅇ 관용여권 소지자 : X ㅇ 일반여권 소지자(복수와 단수를 구분할 경우 별도 표기 필요) : X ㅇ 도착사증 발급 여부 : 정부 대표단 및 기업인이 나이지리아 거래상대방 기업인을 통해 나이지리아 이민청에 긴급한 사유를 설명하고 도착비자 허가레터를 받는경우에 한해 예외적으로 허용 입국시 유의사항 등 해당 없음 출국시 유의사항 등 해당 없음 참고자료 ㅇ 해당 국가의 주한공관홈페이지 : www.nigerianembassy.or.kr ㅇ 해당국가의 한국 대사관 홈페이지 : www.overseas.mofa.go.kr/ng-ko/index.do ㅇ 기타 참고자료 : - 해당국 외교부 : https://foreignaffairs.gov.ng - 해당국 이민청

themes.heading: 종교 관련
themes.text: ㅇ 무슬림을 위한 모스크와 기독교인을 위한 교회가 혼재하고 있으며, 보코하람 등 이슬람 극단주의 무장단체가 모스크와 교회를 구분하지 않고 테러 공격을 하는 경향이 있어 종교시설 출입시 각별히 주의하셔야 합니다. ㅇ 또한, 종교 관련 각종 기념일 전후 테러 발생 가능성에 대해서도 유의하셔야 합니다. ㅇ 무슬림은 이슬람 계율에 따라 정해진 시간마다 장소에 상관없이 기도를 하는 것이 보편적입니다. 일반 여행객들은 이를 자연스럽게 받아들여야 하며 조롱하는 언행을 하거나 기도자 앞으로 지나가는 행동을 삼가야 합니다. ㅇ 특히, 무슬림에 대하여 특정 종교 선교활동은 해당 교도들의 절대적 반감을 일으킬 우려가 있으므로 각별히 주의하셔야 합니다. ㅇ 지방의 경우 현대적인 법체계가 정착되지 않고, 현지 부족의 관습 및 부족장의 권위와 명령에 의해 질서가 유지되는 경우가 많으므로 현지의 관습을 존중해야 합니다.

themes.heading: 팁 문화
themes.text: ㅇ 계산서에 기본적으로 10%의 서비스 이용료가 포함되어 있는 경우가 대부분입니다.

themes.heading: 주요 교통 법규 및 문화
themes.text: ㅇ 자동차 운전석 위치가 한국과 동일합니다. ㅇ 수도 아부자 등 도시에서는 신호등이 설치되어 있으며, 한국과 달리 우회전 신호가 별도로 설치되어 있는 곳에서는 우회전 신호 시에만 우회전이 가능합니다. ㅇ 아부자와 라고스의 경우 도로망이 갖추어져 있으나, 이외 지역의 경우 부실한 도로환경으로 인해 사망사고 등 대형사고의 위험이 아주 높습니다. ㅇ 도시 외곽지역 도로는 상황이 열악합니다. △ 아스팔트 손상 △ pot hole △ 사고차량․고장차량 도로 방치 △ 길 가장자리 개방형 하수도 △ 역주행 차량 △ 소․양 등 방목 가축의 도로 횡단 및 이동 등으로 인해 위험하므로 반드시 서행해야 합니다. ㅇ 도시 외곽에서는 군경의 검문이 수시로 이루어지고 있으며, 운전 중 검문에 응할 경우 자연스럽게 응하고, 의심을 받을 수 있는 행동을 삼가시기 바랍니다. ㅇ 무보험 차량이 많으므로 사전에 국내에서 여행자 보험에 가입하고, 항상 교통안전에 유의하여야 합니다. ㅇ 무엇보다도 야간 운전을 자제해야 합니다. ㅇ 승용차 이외에 외국인이 이용할 수 있는 대중교통수단이 전무(도시 간 항공편 제외) 한 실정입니다. ㅇ 국내선 항공 : 아래 사이트에서 노선을 확인할 수 있으며, 결항 및 지연 운행이 잦습니다. - Arik Peace : www.flyairpeace.com - Arik air :www.arikair.com - Dana air : www.flydana.com - Aero air : www.flyaero.com

themes.heading: 관련 사건사고 사례
themes.text: ㅇ 수도 Abuja 및 경제도시 Lagos에서 서행 및 정차 중인 차량 대상 강도 사례가 지속 발생하고 있습니다. 치안상황 악화로 인해 주요도로에서조차 무장강도가 도로를 차단, 강도·납치 사례가 있으므로 장거리 이동 시 도로 안전상황 사전 확인이 필요합니다. - 2021년 우리 국민 피해 사례 ㅇ 전지역 치안상황 악화로 인해 주요도로에서조차 무장강도가 도로를 차단, 강도·납치 사례가 지속 발생하고 있으니, 육로 이동 시 도로 안전상황 사전 확인 및 개인신변 안전대책을 강구해야 합니다.

themes.heading: 관련 사건사고 발생시 대처방법
themes.text: ㅇ 교통사고 발생 시 긴급전화 112(경찰, 소방, 응급의료)에 신고하고 재외공관 긴급연락처로 연락하십시오.

themes.heading: 기타 유의사항
themes.text: ㅇ 우기철(5월~10월)에 홍수로 인한 대규모 사상자 발생, 고립, 도로 유실 사례가 많으므로 우기철에는 사전 도로상황 확인이 필요합니다. ㅇ 교통사고로 인한 중상해 발생 시 현지 열악한 의료시설로 인해 적절한 처치를 신속히 받지 못할 가능성이 항상 있으므로 안전사고 예방 노력이 필요합니다. ㅇ 운전사 고용이 필수요건인 렌터카 회사가 많습니다. ㅇ 음주측정기를 이용한 음주단속을 상시적으로 실시하지는 않으나, 음주운전으로 인한 교통사고가 의심될 시 경찰의 채혈 요구가 있을 수 있습니다.

themes.heading: 날씨
themes.text: ㅇ 나이지리아는 아프리카 서부에 위치하고 있으며, 남부 지방은 열대우림 기후, 북부지방은 사바나 기후입니다. ㅇ 건기(12~5월), 우기(6~11월) ㅇ 건기에는 평균 섭씨 30도 이상 상승 ㅇ 12~2월 사하라 사막에서 건조하고 더우며 미세먼지가 날아오는 '하마탄' 현상이 발생

themes.heading: 기타
themes.text: ㅇ 황열병 예방접종이 반드시 필요하며, 말라리아 예방약을 사전 복용하는 것이 바람직합니다. ㅇ 이슬람 극단주의 무장단체, 유전지역 무장단체의 테러 위험이 상존하는 지역이므로 사전 철저한 신변안전 조치가 필요합니다.
